Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I believe the issue goes deeper than this: you cannot let the file be closed while the producer is still running. If the module is unloaded after that, the next function call the producer does will Oops.

Using kthread_stop()/kthread_should_stop() is the obvious clean fix but llog_cat_process() is probably going to run interference.

In the end, chlg_release() should explicitely call crs_free, and the reference counter is not needed.

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

> I believe the issue goes deeper than this: you cannot let the file be closed while the producer is still running. If the module is unloaded after that, the next function call the producer does will Oops.

> Using kthread_stop()/kthread_should_stop() is the obvious clean fix but llog_cat_process() is probably going to run interference.

Would it be simpler to take a reference on the module when we allocate crs in chlg_open() and release the reference in crs_free()?

Patch Set 2:

> Would it be simpler to take a reference on the module when we allocate crs in chlg_open() and release the reference in crs_free()?

Is it really a problem to stop the producer thread (and wait for it) from chlg_release()?
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

A simple kthread_stop / kthread_should_stop seems to work properly...
Thanks Quentin for the good catch, I must admit I haven't even considered that the module could be deregistered concurrently to this :)

Patch Set 3:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

> (...) but llog_cat_process() is probably going to run interference

I was wrong, llog_cat_process_or_fork() is never called with "fork = true", so using kthread_stop() / kthread_should_stop() works like a charm.

On another matter, testing the patch I noticed this in the output of dmesg:
[ 4263.933580] LustreError: 4740:0:(mdc_changelog.c:244:chlg_load()) lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff880c507cf000: fail to process llog: rc = -5

(the test was a basic: lctl chlg_register -> touch / rm file -> lfs chlg)

It does not seem related to the patch (happens on master too) but I assume it is worth investigating.

Patch Set 5:

this patch makes the system crash (but patchset 2 was stable)

  [52379.112080] BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at           (null)

  [52379.388356]  [<ffffffff8108422a>] __put_task_struct+0x4a/0x140
  [52379.394864]  [<ffffffff810b0c47>] kthread_stop+0x67/0xe0
  [52379.400800]  [<ffffffffc0db42b3>] chlg_release+0x23/0x1e0 [mdc]
  [52379.407405]  [<ffffffff81202fc9>] __fput+0xe9/0x260
  [52379.412844]  [<ffffffff8120322e>] ____fput+0xe/0x10
  [52379.418287]  [<ffffffff810ad247>] task_work_run+0xa7/0xf0
  [52379.424310]  [<ffffffff8102ab62>] do_notify_resume+0x92/0xb0
  [52379.430625]  [<ffffffff816b52bd>] int_signal+0x12/0x17

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Oh! Indeed, I completely forgot, kthread_stop() must only be called on a running thread (the thread must not exit on its own). Should have caught it much earlier, this isn't the first time I work with it. Really sorry.

commit.subject: LU-10166 mdc: invalid free in changelog reader
commit.message:
LU-10166 mdc: invalid free in changelog reader

Use kthread_stop() to instruct the producer thread to exit
when the device is closed, and only then: release the CRS
structure.

The previous implementation left small time windows open,
during which the producer threads could free a structure
before the consumer thread was completely done with it.
